name: South China Sea Sweep

environmental_profile.notes: Short steep chop. Typhoon residual swell intersects with monsoon wind-waves at oblique angles. Shallow continental shelf amplifies wave height.

rationale: The South China Sea tests everything at once. Short-period steep chop accumulates fatigue faster than big Southern Ocean swells. Warm high-salinity water runs corrosion in parallel. Residual typhoon cross-swell attacks stability from oblique angles. The weakest link in the configuration fails first.
